prerequisites: A New York Times account, used to sign in to the NYT Developer Portal at developer.nytimes.com.
A registered app in the Developer Portal with the relevant API enabled, which yields the api_key.
A period value (number of days; the Most Popular API supports 1, 7, or 30) for the required period config field.
A start_date for the required start_date config field; optionally an end_date to bound the sync window.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a single api_key secret. NYT APIs require an api-key passed as a query parameter on every request. You obtain the key by registering an app in the NYT Developer Portal and enabling the API for that app.

setupSteps.title: Sign in to the NYT Developer Portal
setupSteps.body: Go to developer.nytimes.com and sign in, or create a free New York Times account.

setupSteps.title: Register an app and enable the API
setupSteps.body: In the portal, create (register) an app and enable the API you want to read from for that app. Enabling the API on the app is what provisions an API key tied to it.

setupSteps.title: Copy the API key
setupSteps.body: Open your app in the portal and copy its API key. Paste this value into the connector's required api_key field (stored as a secret).

setupSteps.title: Configure the read window and parameters
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date and period fields (period is the number of days, for example 1, 7, or 30 for the Most Popular API). Optionally set end_date to bound the sync and share_type where the endpoint supports it (for example a share type such as facebook for most-shared content).

sources.title: New York Times APIs | NYT Developer Portal
